commit.message:
feat: add auth/jwt package — local JWT validation for microservices

Moved from auth-service/pkg/authclient. Allows billing-service and
company-service to validate JWTs without a go.mod replace directive
pointing to auth-service source code.

Includes Claims struct, LocalValidator, and 8 unit tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: StrataChart fills all available horizontal space

Charts now override MeasureOverride to request the full available
width, so parent containers (e.g. left-aligned chat bubbles) expand
properly instead of collapsing to near-zero when the chart is the
only content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(lemonade): switch default API to Ollama-compatible on port 8000

Use api: "ollama" instead of "openai-completions" for the lemonade
provider. This makes model listing, loading, and downloading work more
reliably since Lemonade's Ollama-compatible API is the preferred path.

Changes:
- Base URL: http://localhost:8000/api/v1 -> http://localhost:8000
- probeLemonadeModels: GET /api/tags (Ollama) instead of GET /models (OpenAI)
  Returns { models: [{name}] } which we map to { id: name }
- pullLemonadeModel: POST /api/pull (Ollama NDJSON) instead of POST /pull (SSE)
  Parses { status, completed, total } progress lines
- applyLemonadeProviderConfig: api: "ollama" instead of "openai-completions"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Cantonese autoplay — derive filename from fields instead of [sound:] tag

Anki auto-plays ALL [sound:] tags in rendered HTML regardless of CSS
display:none. Removed {{CantoneseAudio}} from templates entirely.
JS now constructs the filename from Hanzi + Jyutping fields already
on the card (pattern: yue_{hanzi}_{jyutping}.mp3), matching the
audio generation convention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Redesign card templates — character-first Design 2

Hero section: large character, keyword subtitle, inline pronunciations
with dot separator (xíng · haang4 🔊). No labels — pattern is obvious.

Example in tinted panel with rounded corners. Story left-aligned in
its own panel. Dropped lesson meta from front, removed all <hr>
dividers in favor of spacing and background zones.

Listening front: large emoji prompt, cleaner hint styling.

Full night mode coverage for all new elements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add odt-convert skill

Converts ODT files to Markdown with comment extraction,
embedded image and Visio diagram support.

commit.message:
test: add dotnet-backend skill evaluation results

Evals workspace with:
- 3 scenarios × 2 conditions (with-skill, baseline)
- 14/14 assertions passing (with-skill) vs 11/14 (baseline)
- +3 point delta: JsonWebTokenHandler, CancellationToken propagation,
  no EF Core bleed
- Optimization report: activation STRONG, benchmark COMPLETE, context
  budget ACCEPTABLE, zero regressions
- All release gates PASS

Scenarios:
1. Products CRUD with Dapper + RFC 9457 Problem Details
2. JWT Bearer auth with role-based authorization
3. Serilog structured logging with LoggerMessage.Define

Result: dotnet-backend skill is production-ready.

feat: add dotnet-backend skill for .NET 10 / C# 14

Comprehensive backend skill covering ASP.NET Core 10 REST APIs with:
- 21 rule files covering minimal APIs, controllers, routing, middleware,
  validation, auth (JWT), authorization, DI, error handling (RFC 9457),
  logging (Serilog), caching, async patterns, performance (native AOT,
  Span<T>), CORS/security/rate limiting, testing (xUnit+Moq),
  integration testing (WebApplicationFactory), database patterns
  (Dapper + ADO.NET — no EF Core), configuration (IOptions),
  deployment (Docker, native AOT), C# 14 features, and built-in
  OpenAPI (no Swashbuckle)
- Evals: 14/14 with-skill vs 11/14 baseline across 3 real-world tasks
- Key differentiators: JsonWebTokenHandler over deprecated
  JwtSecurityTokenHandler, no EF Core bleed, modern C# 14 patterns

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: rename all test methods to Subject_Result_WhenCondition

WhenCondition is omitted when self-evident from Subject or Result.

Tests renamed (40 total across 5 files):

ConcurrencyTests (2)
  EveryTrigger_ConcurrentCalls_*  → EveryTrigger_*_WhenCalledConcurrently
  OnceTrigger_ConcurrentCalls_*   → OnceTrigger_*_WhenCalledConcurrently

LoopTests (10)
  RunAsync_Once_*                 → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingOnceTrigger
  RunAsync_After_*                → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingAfterTrigger
  RunAsync_Every_*                → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingEveryTrigger
  RunAsync_Cancellation*          → RunAsync_*_WhenCancelled*
  RunAsync_ErrorPolicy*           → RunAsync_*_WhenErrorPolicy*
  RunAsync_CustomOnError_*        → RunAsync_*_WhenCustomErrorHandler*
  RunAsync_WhenAny_*              → RunAsync_*_WhenUsingWhenAnyTrigger

TriggerTests (16) — WhenCondition dropped where obvious:
  Once_FiresOnce                  → Once_ReturnsNullOnSecondCall
  Once_FiresImmediately           → Once_ReturnsTimeCloseToNow
  After_FiresOnceAfterDelay       → After_SchedulesAfterDelayAndExhausts
  Every_FiresRepeatedly           → Every_SchedulesOnFixedInterval
  WhenAny_PicksEarliest           → WhenAny_ReturnsEarliestTime
  WhenAll_PicksLatest             → WhenAll_ReturnsLatestTime
  (others retain WhenCondition to disambiguate)

LoggingMiddlewareTests (3), RetryMiddlewareTests (6),
MiddlewarePipelineTests (3) — same rules applied.

Docs: CONTRIBUTING.md — new Test naming section with table, examples,
      and explicit guidance on when WhenCondition can be omitted.
      AGENTS.md — one-liner in Conventions pointing at CONTRIBUTING.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: lock AssemblyVersion to major, FileVersion to full numeric version

Adds src/Directory.Build.targets (imported after SDK targets, so $(Version)
is fully resolved) with two overrides for all library projects:

  AssemblyVersion = major.0.0.0
    Locked to the major version so consumers compiled against v1.0 don't
    need to recompile for v1.1/v1.2 (binding-redirect / strong-name safety).

  FileVersion = major.minor.patch.0
    Pre-release label stripped (regex match on '^\d+\.\d+\.\d+') because
    Windows file-version fields only accept numeric components.

  InformationalVersion = x.y.z[-pre]+<commit-sha>
    Left to the SDK default — carries the full SemVer label plus source hash.

Verified against three cases:
  -p:Version=1.2.3      → AV=1.0.0.0  FV=1.2.3.0   IV=1.2.3+sha
  -p:Version=2.0.0-beta.1 → AV=2.0.0.0  FV=2.0.0.0   IV=2.0.0-beta.1+sha
  default (0.1.0)       → AV=0.0.0.0  FV=0.1.0.0   IV=0.1.0+sha

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GitHub Pages deployment workflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(snap): add PYTHONPATH so python3 venv/ensurepip/pip resolve correctly

The snap stages python3, python3-pip, and python3.12-venv but does not set
PYTHONPATH, so the snap's python3 binary cannot find its own ensurepip or
pip modules when skill setup scripts call python3 -m venv.

Add PYTHONPATH=$SNAP/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages:$SNAP/usr/lib/python3.12
to both the ubuclaw app and the service daemon environment so that any
python3 invocation from within the snap finds the staged libraries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add NuGet packaging and publish pipeline

Directory.Build.props (root)
- Centralises TargetFramework, Nullable, ImplicitUsings for all projects
- Sets IsPackable=false and GenerateDocumentationFile=false as repo defaults

src/Directory.Build.props
- Overrides IsPackable=true, GenerateDocumentationFile=true for library projects
- Enables IncludeSymbols + SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg
- Shared NuGet metadata: Authors, PackageTags, RepositoryUrl, PackageReadmeFile
- Bundles README.md into every package; VersionPrefix=0.1.0

csproj changes
- src/Looop: add PackageId=KatzuoOgust.Looop, Description; remove properties now
  in Directory.Build.props
- src/Looop.AspNetCore: same, PackageId=KatzuoOgust.Looop.AspNetCore
- tests/Looop.Tests: remove IsPackable=false (root default), TargetFramework,
  Nullable, ImplicitUsings
- examples/*: remove TargetFramework, Nullable, ImplicitUsings

XML doc fixes (surfaced by GenerateDocumentationFile=true)
- ITrigger.NextAsync: add param/return comments
- IJobMiddleware: fix broken <paramref> on delegate, add InvokeAsync comment
- MiddlewareAwareJob: add constructor summary

Makefile: add pack and push targets
CI: add Pack (verify) step after tests
publish.yml: new workflow triggered on v*.*.* tags;
  strips leading 'v' for dotnet pack -p:Version, pushes .nupkg + .snupkg

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(ui): enforce _base ownership across version components

Complete architecture sweep so version components are theme wrappers over _base mechanics. Add missing _base bridges for special surfaces (toaster, empty-state, status-bar, circuit-decor, effects) and rewire version wrappers accordingly. Ensure sonner-based toaster path and retain themed styling.

Validated with architecture audit plus lint, typecheck, and build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(snap): expand ~ to absolute path in display and skill paths

Inside the snap sandbox HOME is set to SNAP_USER_COMMON, not the user's
real home directory. Using ~ in displayed commands or paths sent to the
model produces paths that break when pasted in a real terminal outside
the sandbox.

- resolveHomeDisplayPrefix(): when process.env.SNAP is set, return the
  full absolute path as the prefix so shortenHomePath/displayPath never
  emit ~ (affects all display call sites automatically)
- setup.finalize.ts: replace hardcoded ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with
  displayPath(resolveConfigDir()/openclaw.json) so it expands correctly
- compactSkillPaths(): skip ~ compaction in snap context so skill file
  paths in the model system prompt remain absolute and copy-paste safe

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix payment error handling: use ErrorLogger, add try/catch for non-critical ops

- Wrap invoice creation and audit logging in try/catch blocks
- Add detailed context (stripe_session_id, IDs) to all error logs
- Change outer catch blocks from Exception to \Throwable
- Keep critical operations (enrollment INSERT, booking UPDATE) unwrapped

commit.message:
Fix memory leaks in StrataChatComposer and MermaidCanvas

StrataChatComposer: Add OnDetachedFromVisualTree to unsubscribe
CollectionChanged handlers on external ViewModel collections
(SkillItems, McpItems, AvailableMcps) that kept the control rooted.

MermaidCanvas: Add OnDetachedFromVisualTree to stop the entrance
animation DispatcherTimer on detach, preventing the canvas and its
parent StrataMermaid from being held alive mid-animation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  Move extra/typescript.lua → lsp/typescript-tools.lua

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(snap): skip systemctl is-enabled when running inside snap

Two related fixes:

1. isSystemdServiceEnabled now returns false immediately when SNAP is set.
   The snap gateway service is managed by snapd (daemon-scope: user), not
   by a user-written .service file, so systemctl is-enabled is meaningless.
   Avoids 'systemctl is-enabled unavailable: Command failed: /snap/.../
   systemctl --user is-enabled openclaw-gateway.service' at the end of
   ubuclaw onboard.

2. isGenericSystemctlIsEnabledFailure now recognises the snap shim path
   pattern 'Command failed: /snap/.../bin/systemctl --user is-enabled ...'
   as a non-fatal probe failure, in addition to the plain
   'Command failed: systemctl ...' pattern for non-snap environments.
   This is a defensive fallback in case the SNAP guard is bypassed.

commit.message:
feat: create complete Flutter POS project skeleton (AMA-POS)

- Package name: tw.kazan.amapos, Android tablet-first
- Database: sqflite with models, DAOs, seed data (no code generation needed)
- State: plain Riverpod StreamProvider/Provider (no @riverpod annotations)
- Navigation: go_router with cashier and backoffice routes
- Cashier: product grid, order panel, dine-in/takeout, checkout flow
- Backoffice: categories, products, shifts, daily reports, import/export
- Android: minSdk 21, targetSdk 34, compileSdk 34, landscape orientation
- Seed data: 3 categories and 12 sample products on first launch
